detaileddescription.para: Create a PixelBuffer with it's own data buffer.
Convert to a pixel data and release the pixelBuffer's object. This handle is left empty.

Any other handles that keep a reference to this object will be left with no buffer, trying to access it will return NULL.
@SINCE_1_2.46
pixelBuffer
a new PixelData which takes ownership of the PixelBuffer's buffer.
Convert to a pixel data and release the pixelBuffer's object. This handle is left empty.

Any other handles that keep a reference to this object will be left with no buffer, trying to access it will return NULL.
@SINCE_2_3.6
pixelBuffer
releaseAfterUpload
Whether converted PixelData released after upload or not.
a new PixelData which takes ownership of the PixelBuffer's buffer.

briefdescription.para: Creates an empty handle. Use PixelBuffer::New() to create an initialized object.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.46
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this object

briefdescription.para: This move constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This move assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_1.45
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this object

Copy the data from this object into a new PixelData object, which could be used for uploading to a texture.
a new PixelData object containing a copy of this pixel buffer's data.

briefdescription.para: Gets the pixel buffer. This is a pointer to the internal pixel buffer.
Gets the pixel buffer. This is a pointer to the internal pixel buffer.

simplesect.para: If there is no pixel buffer (e.g. this object has been converted to a PixelData), this method will return NULL.

briefdescription.para: Gets the width of the buffer in pixels.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.46
The width of the buffer in pixels

briefdescription.para: Gets the height of the buffer in pixels.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.46
The height of the buffer in pixels

briefdescription.para: Gets the stride of the buffer in pixels.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_1.17 @DEPRECATED_2_4.8 Use GetStrideBytes() instead.
The stride of the buffer in pixels. 0 means the buffer is tightly packed.

briefdescription.para: Gets the stride of the buffer in bytes.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_4.8
The stride of the buffer in bytes. 0 means the buffer is tightly packed.

briefdescription.para: Gets the pixel format.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.46
The pixel format

Apply the mask to this pixel data, and return a new pixel data containing the masked image. If this PixelBuffer doesn't have an alpha channel, then the resultant PixelBuffer will be converted to a format that supports at least the width of the color channels and the alpha channel from the mask.

If cropToMask is set to true, then the contentScale is applied first to this buffer, and the target buffer is cropped to the size of the mask. If it's set to false, then the mask is scaled to match this buffer's size before the mask is applied.

parameterdescription.para: The scaling factor to apply to the content
Whether to crop the output to the mask size (true) or scale the mask to the content size (false)

detaileddescription.para: Apply a Gaussian blur to this pixel data with the given radius.

simplesect.para: A bigger radius will yield a blurrier image.

parameterdescription.para: The radius for Gaussian blur. A value of 0 or negative value indicates no blur.

simplesect.para: false if the gaussian blur fails (invalid pixel format or memory issues).

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t x, uint16_t y, uint16_t width, uint16_t height)
memberdef.name: Crop

briefdescription.para: Crops this buffer to the given crop rectangle.

detaileddescription.para:
The crop rectangle will be clamped to the edges of the buffer if it is larger.
x
The top left corner's X
y
The top left corner's y
width
The crop width
height
The crop height

briefdescription.para: Resizes the buffer to the given dimensions.

detaileddescription.para:
Uses either Lanczos4 for downscaling or Mitchell for upscaling
width
The new width
height
The new height

briefdescription.para: Returns Exif metadata as a property map.

parameterdescription.para: Property map object to write into

briefdescription.para: Multiplies the image's color values by the alpha value. This provides better blending capability.

simplesect.para: Compressed pixel format doesn't have any efforts.
Even if pixel format doesn't have an alpha channel, it will be marked as PreMultiplied.

briefdescription.para: Rotates the pixel buffer by the given angle.

simplesect.para: Operation valid for pixel formats: A8, L8, LA88, RGB888, RGB8888, BGR8888, RGBA8888 and BGRA8888. Fails otherwise.
The operation does nothing for angles equivalent to 0 degrees: -360, 360, 720, etc.
If the pixel buffer does rotate, all the pointers to the internal pixel buffer retrieved by the method GetPixelBuffer() become invalid.
false if the rotation fails (invalid pixel format or memory issues).

briefdescription.para: Returns pixel-buffer is premultiplied or not.
Get the brightness of the pixel buffer.

simplesect.para: The range is 255 to 0. The closer to 255, the brighter. 0 is not bright.

briefdescription.para: The constructor.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a newly allocated PixelBuffer

briefdescription.para: The PixelBuffer object holds a pixel buffer.

detaileddescription.para: The PixelBuffer keeps ownership of it's initial buffer however, the user is free to modify the pixel data, either directly, or via image operations.

In order to upload the pixel data to texture memory, there are two possibilities - either convert it back to a PixelData object, which releases the PixelBuffer object, leaving the user with an empty handle (ideal for one-time indirect image manipulation), or create a new PixelData object from this object, leaving the buffer intact (ideal for continuous manipulation)
